<summary>Agent "Map the tick/move/eat/collision loop" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>I have everything needed. Here is the precise map of the per-tick pipeline in `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts`.

## 1. Dispatcher and per-tick order of operations

### `update()` â€” lines 1384-1392 (dispatcher)
```ts
private update() {
  if (this.isPaused || this.isGameOver) return
  if (this.isTwoSnakeMode()) {
    this.updateTwoPlayer()
  } else {
    this.updateSinglePlayer()
  }
}
```

### `updateSinglePlayer()` â€” lines 1394-1455
Order within a tick:
1. Bot direction (if `botEnabled`) â€” 1395-1400
2. **Direction application** `this.direction = this.nextDirection` â€” 1402
3. **Compute new head cell** (clone head[0], mutate x/y by direction) â€” 1403-1410
4. **Collision check** (wall/board-mask + self) via `checkCollision(head)` â€” 1412-1424 (early return on death)
5. **Apply move**: `unshift(head)` + `snakeSet.add` â€” 1426-1427
6. **Food-eating check + growth + score** â€” 1429-1439
7. maxLength bookkeeping â€” 1441
8. **Starvation check** â€” 1443-1449
9. draw/updateUI â€” 1451-1454

```ts
this.direction = this.nextDirection
const head = { ...this.snake[0] }
switch (this.direction) {
  case 'UP': head.y--; break
  case 'DOWN': head.y++; break
  case 'LEFT': head.x--; break
  case 'RIGHT': head.x++; break
}

if (this.checkCollision(head)) {
  const insideGrid = head.x >= 0 && head.x < this.gridSize && head.y >= 0 && head.y < this.gridSize
  this.deathMessage = !insideGrid ? 'Hit the wall!'
    : !this.inBounds(head) ? 'Fell into a hole!'
    : 'Ran into itself!'
  this.deathCells = [{ x: head.x, y: head.y, who: 1 }]
  this.beginDeathFreeze(() => this.endGame())
  return
}

this.snake.unshift(head)
this.snakeSet.add(`${head.x},${head.y}`)

if (head.x === this.food.x && head.y === this.food.y) {
  this.score++
  this.movesSinceFood1 = 0
  resetLoopMemory(this.botLoopMemory)
  this.spawnFood()
  this.checkGridExpansion()
} else {
  this.movesSinceFood1++
  const tail = this.snake.pop()!
  this.snakeSet.delete(`${tail.x},${tail.y}`)
}
```
Note: growth is implicit â€” on eat the tail is NOT popped (head already unshifted); on non-eat the tail is popped. `checkGridExpansion()` is called only inside the eat branch (line 1434), i.e. **every eat**.

Key insight for your hook: the head cell is fully known at line 1410 and validated (on-board, non-colliding) by line 1424, before the food check at 1429. In Iron Snake mode `checkGridExpansion()` just calls `checkLevelAdvance()`.

### `updateTwoPlayer()` â€” lines 1457-1574
Same order, doubled:
- Bot dirs (bvb) â€” 1459-1464
- Direction application â€” 1466-1467
- **Compute heads** via `DIRECTION_VECTORS` (not a switch) â€” 1470-1478
- **Collision detection** (off-board, self, other, head-to-head) â€” 1480-1520
- **Apply moves** â€” 1522-1526
- **Food check + score** â€” 1528-1541 (`checkGridExpansion()` at 1540, once, if either ate)
- tail removal â€” 1543-1551
- maxLength â€” 1553-1554
- starvation â€” 1556-1569
- draw/updateUI â€” 1571-1573

```ts
this.snake.unshift(head1)
this.snakeSet.add(`${head1.x},${head1.y}`)
this.snake2.unshift(head2)
this.snakeSet2.add(`${head2.x},${head2.y}`)

const p1AteFood = head1.x === this.food.x && head1.y === this.food.y
const p2AteFood = head2.x === this.food.x && head2.y === this.food.y

if (p1AteFood) this.score++
if (p2AteFood) this.score2++
this.movesSinceFood1 = p1AteFood ? 0 : this.movesSinceFood1 + 1
this.movesSinceFood2 = p2AteFood ? 0 : this.movesSinceFood2 + 1
if (p1AteFood || p2AteFood) {
  resetLoopMemory(this.botLoopMemory)
  resetLoopMemory(this.botLoopMemory2)
  this.spawnFood()
  this.checkGridExpansion()
}
```

## 2. Food eating detection & effects
- Single: line 1429 `if (head.x === this.food.x && head.y === this.food.y)` â†’ `score++` (1430), `movesSinceFood1 = 0` (1431, starvation reset), `spawnFood()` (1433), `checkGridExpansion()` (1434). Growth by omission of tail-pop.
- Two-player: lines 1529-1530 compute `p1AteFood`/`p2AteFood`; scores at 1532-1533; starvation reset via ternary at 1534-1535; `spawnFood()`+`checkGridExpansion()` once if either ate (1536-1541); tails popped only for the snake(s) that didn't eat (1544-1551).
- `checkGridExpansion` is called on every eat (both paths).

## 3. Wall / off-board collision detection

### `onBoard(x, y)` â€” lines 1680-1684 (single source of truth for board extent)
```ts
private onBoard(x: number, y: number): boolean {
  if (x < 0 || x >= this.gridSize || y < 0 || y >= this.gridSize) return false
  if (this.boardMask === null) return true
  return this.boardMask[y * this.gridSize + x] === 1
}
```

### `inBounds(position)` â€” lines 1686-1688 (delegates to `onBoard`)
```ts
private inBounds(position: Position): boolean {
  return this.onBoard(position.x, position.y)
}
```

### `wouldCollide(head, snake, blockedSet?)` â€” lines 1765-1773
```ts
private wouldCollide(head: Position, snake: Position[], blockedSet?: Set<string>): boolean {
  if (!this.inBounds(head)) return true
  if (blockedSet) return blockedSet.has(`${head.x},${head.y}`)
  return snake.some(segment => segment.x === head.x && segment.y === head.y)
}
```

### `checkCollision(head)` â€” lines 1841-1843 (single-player wrapper)
```ts
private checkCollision(head: Position): boolean {
  return this.wouldCollide(head, this.snake, this.snakeSet)
}
```
The two-player path does NOT use `checkCollision`; it inlines `!this.inBounds(headN)` plus `snakeSet`/`snakeSet2` lookups (lines 1483-1497). `boardMask` field declared at line 130.

## 4. `spawnFood()` â€” lines 2208-2243
Two strategies. When a mask is active (`this.boardMask !== null`) or the board is >half full, it **enumerates** on-board empty cells (respecting `onBoard`, excluding both snake sets) and picks uniformly (2214-2230). Otherwise it does rejection sampling over the raw grid (2232-2242), which does NOT consult `onBoard` (fine only when mask is null).
```ts
if (this.boardMask !== null || combinedLength > this.boardArea * 0.5) {
  const emptyCells: Position[] = []
  for (let y = 0; y < this.gridSize; y++) {
    for (let x = 0; x < this.gridSize; x++) {
      if (!this.onBoard(x, y)) continue
      const key = `${x},${y}`
      if (!this.snakeSet.has(key) && (!this.isTwoSnakeMode() || !this.snakeSet2.has(key))) {
        emptyCells.push({ x, y })
      }
    }
  }
  if (emptyCells.length > 0) {
    this.food = emptyCells[Math.floor(Math.random() * emptyCells.length)]
  }
  return
}
```
So the enumerate-on-board pattern (loop over grid, skip `!onBoard`) is the model you'd mirror to place an EXIT on a valid cell.

## 5. Helpers for on-board cell listing / border/edge detection

- **No existing "border/edge cell of the playable region" helper anywhere in src/.** No function detects whether an on-board cell touches the outer void.
- `sortedCandidates(preferred, occupied)` â€” main.ts lines 1697-1712 â€” enumerates all on-board, unoccupied cells sorted by Manhattan distance. Closest existing "list on-board cells" helper in `main.ts`.
- `onBoardCells(mask)` â€” **iron-snake.ts lines 170-176** â€” returns flat indices where `mask[i] === 1`. This is **module-private (not exported)**; only `generateIronSnakeBoard`, `growIronSnakeBoard`, `ironSnakeGridSizeForArea` are exported (iron-snake.ts lines 311, 369, 445; imported at main.ts line 16). You cannot call `onBoardCells` from main.ts as-is.
- `NEIGHBORS` (4-directional offsets) â€” iron-snake.ts line 55 â€” used internally for connectivity/hole logic; would be the pattern for "cell adjacent to an off-board cell = border", but it's module-private to iron-snake.ts.
- To detect a border cell you'd combine `onBoard(x,y) === true` with at least one orthogonal neighbor where `onBoard === false` (the outer grid edge, since `onBoard` returns false for x/y outside `[0,gridSize)`, also counts as a border). No such combined check currently exists.

## 6. Single vs two-player & level-advance scoring

### `isTwoSnakeMode()` â€” lines 261-263
```ts
private isTwoSnakeMode(): boolean {
  return this.gameMode === 'pvp' || this.gameMode === 'bvb'
}
```
`gameMode: GameMode` field at line 105.

### `checkGridExpansion()` â€” lines 1887-1902; in Iron Snake it delegates to `checkLevelAdvance()`:
```ts
private checkGridExpansion() {
  if (this.ironSnakeMode) {
    this.checkLevelAdvance()
    return
  }
  ...
}
```

### `checkLevelAdvance()` â€” lines 1974-1979 (uses COMBINED score)
```ts
private checkLevelAdvance() {
  const combined = this.score + (this.isTwoSnakeMode() ? this.score2 : 0)
  if (combined >= this.levelGoal) {
    this.advanceLevel()
  }
}
```
Yes â€” in two-player it sums both snakes' scores against `this.levelGoal`. Same combined-score logic in `ironSnakeRemaining()` (lines 1865-1869) which drives the HUD "remaining" display.

### Advancement chain
`advanceLevel()` (1981-1997) snapshots the old mask, calls `applyLevelAdvance()` (2002+: `level++`, resize grid via `ironSnakeGridSizeForLevel`, translate snakes, `growIronSnakeBoard`, `spawnFood`), then stops the loop and starts a morph animation (`startLevelMorph`, restarted by `finishLevelMorph` at 2168). Relevant Iron Snake state fields: `ironSnakeMode` (129), `boardMask` (130), `boardArea` (131), `levelGoal` (132), `level` (133).

## Summary for your JOE-9 hook
The natural interception point is where the goal is currently checked instantly: `checkLevelAdvance()` (1974) is invoked from `checkGridExpansion()` (1891) on every eat. Today reaching `combined >= levelGoal` calls `advanceLevel()` immediately. For an exit-cell mechanic you'd need (a) a place to spawn/track the exit once the goal is met rather than advancing (near lines 1890-1892 / 1974-1978), and (b) a per-tick check that the new head landed on the exit cell â€” the head is computed and validated at lines 1403-1424 (single) and 1470-1520 (two-player), with the food-equality comparison at 1429 / 1529-1530 being the exact structural analog for an "head === exit" test. There is no existing border-cell helper, and `onBoardCells` is not exported from iron-snake.ts.</result>

<result>I have everything I need. Here is the complete rendering map for the JOE-9 Exit feature.

## Overview

`src/main.ts` is one class `SnakeGame`. All rendering is canvas-based and goes through a single `draw()` method (line 735) that runs for both isometric and overhead views. There is no DOM/SVG rendering of board cells â€” only HUD text and banners are DOM. The board is an irregular mask (`boardMask`) with `onBoard()` as the single source of truth for playable cells.

---

## 1. `draw()` and its call tree (line 735-861)

`draw()` clears the canvas, then calls the ground helpers, collects all objects into a depth-sorted list, draws shadows, then draws blocks.

Top of `draw()` (lines 735-742):
```ts
private draw() {
  const ctx = this.ctx
  ctx.fillStyle = '#1a1a1a'
  ctx.fillRect(0, 0, this.canvas.width, this.canvas.height)

  this.drawGroundPlane()   // line 348
  this.drawGridLines()     // line 378
  this.drawGroundBorder()  // line 431
```

Object collection â€” note food is pushed as a single object at line 764:
```ts
type DrawType = 'head' | 'body' | 'tail' | 'food' | 'head2' | 'body2' | 'tail2' | 'death'
const objects: { x: number; y: number; type: DrawType; seg?: number; segLen?: number }[] = []
...
objects.push({ x: this.food.x, y: this.food.y, type: 'food' })   // line 764
```

Painter's-algorithm depth sort (back-to-front) at lines 787-789:
```ts
objects.sort((a, b) =>
  (a.x * this.basisXy + a.y * this.basisYy) - (b.x * this.basisXy + b.y * this.basisYy)
)
```

Shadows for every object (line 792-794), then the block switch (lines 821-850). The FOOD case (lines 843-845):
```ts
case 'food':
  this.drawBlock(obj.x, obj.y, foodColors[0], foodColors[1], foodColors[2])
  break
```

So food is just a `drawBlock` (an extruded isometric prism) using `foodColors`. There is no distinct food "shape" â€” it is a normal cell block, distinguished only by color.

`drawGroundPlane()` (lines 348-376): fills each on-board cell as a flat iso diamond using `isoCache` corners, checkerboard `#2a2a2a` / `#222222`; skips cells where `boardMask[...] !== 1`.

Isometric projection helpers:
- `toIso(gridX, gridY)` (lines 273-282): grid coords â†’ screen point, applying rotation basis + perspective.
- `isoCache[gy][gx]` (built lines 338-345): precomputed `toIso` for every lattice intersection point; used by ground/grid/border drawing.

---

## 2. Final-three-fruits recoloring (JOE-6 countdown)

Color table (lines 59-63):
```ts
const IRON_SNAKE_FINAL_FRUIT_COLORS: Record<number, [string, string, string]> = {
  3: ['#8b5cf6', '#7c3aed', '#6d28d9'], // violet
  2: ['#ec4899', '#db2777', '#be185d'], // magenta
  1: ['#f8fafc', '#e2e8f0', '#cbd5e1'], // white flash (final fruit)
}
```

Read inside `draw()` at lines 815-818 â€” it calls `ironSnakeRemaining()` and indexes the table, defaulting to red when not in the final stretch:
```ts
// Iron Snake final-stretch cue: recolour the fruit for the last three fruits.
const remainingFruit = this.ironSnakeRemaining()
const foodColors: [string, string, string] =
  IRON_SNAKE_FINAL_FRUIT_COLORS[remainingFruit] ?? ['#ef4444', '#dc2626', '#b91c1c']
```

`ironSnakeRemaining()` (lines 1865-1869):
```ts
private ironSnakeRemaining(): number {
  if (!this.ironSnakeMode) return 0
  const combined = this.score + (this.isTwoSnakeMode() ? this.score2 : 0)
  return Math.max(0, this.levelGoal - combined)
}
```
So when `remainingFruit` reaches 0 (goal met), the table lookup misses and food falls back to red â€” this is the exact moment your EXIT would appear.

---

## 3. Single-cell / block draw helpers (reuse these for the exit)

`drawBlock(gx, gy, topColor, rightColor, leftColor, heightScale = 1)` (lines 513-524) â€” the primary reusable helper. Builds the four inset ground corners and calls `drawPrism`:
```ts
private drawBlock(gx: number, gy: number, topColor: string, rightColor: string, leftColor: string, heightScale: number = 1) {
  const inset = 0.05
  const bh = this.getBlockHeight(gx, gy) * heightScale
  // Corners clockwise: TL, TR, BR, BL
  const c = [
    this.toIso(gx + inset, gy + inset),
    this.toIso(gx + 1 - inset, gy + inset),
    this.toIso(gx + 1 - inset, gy + 1 - inset),
    this.toIso(gx + inset, gy + 1 - inset)
  ]
  this.drawPrism(c, bh, topColor, rightColor, leftColor)
}
```

`drawPrism(c, bh, topColor, rightColor, leftColor)` (lines 530-579) â€” draws the extruded 3-face prism (back faces, front faces with light/dark selection, then top). This is the shared shading primitive; `drawBlock`, `drawSnakeHead`, and `drawSnakeTail` all funnel through it.

Supporting helpers:
- `getBlockHeight(gx, gy)` (lines 487-493): perspective-corrected extrusion height; returns `baseBlockHeight` scaled. Returns 0-height in overhead view (since `baseBlockHeight = 0`).
- `drawBlockShadow(gx, gy)` (lines 495-511): flat translucent diamond drawn under a cell; called for every object at line 793.
- `drawSnakeHead` (588-626) and `drawSnakeTail` (640-661) are specializations if you want a fancier exit; but for a door/portal cell, `drawBlock` is the direct analog to how food is drawn.

The three-shade convention is (top lightest, right mid, left darkest), matching the constant palettes and `rainbowFaces` (lines 730-733).

---

## 4. Overhead vs isometric â€” single code path

There is one draw path. Overhead is not a separate renderer; it changes camera params so the same `drawBlock`/`drawPrism` code produces flat squares.

`toggleOverheadView()` (lines 687-705) zeroes the angle/perspective:
```ts
this.isoAngle = 0
this.perspectiveRatio = 1
this.perspectiveStrength = 0
```

And `updateCanvasSize()` line 301 flattens block height in overhead:
```ts
this.baseBlockHeight = this.overheadView ? 0 : scale * pr * Math.SQRT2 * 0.6
```

Consequence: any cell drawn via `drawBlock` renders as a flat filled diamond in overhead automatically (bh = 0, so `drawPrism` draws only the top face). `drawSnakeHead` explicitly checks `if (bh <= 0) return` (line 593) to skip the vertical-face eyes in overhead. So a new EXIT drawn with `drawBlock` needs no overhead-specific branch â€” it will Just Work in both views. If you draw glow/detail on a vertical face, guard it with the same `bh <= 0` check.

For identifying a border cell of the irregular board, see `onBoard(x, y)` (lines 1680-1684) and the outline-tracing logic in `drawGroundBorder()` (lines 454-484), which marks a cell edge as border when the neighbor across it is off-board.

---

## 5. HUD / on-screen status

`updateUI()` (lines 2247-2269) writes the DOM HUD:
```ts
document.getElementById('score')!.textContent = this.score.toString()     // (or p1-score/p2-score in 2P)
document.getElementById('level')!.textContent = this.level.toString()
document.getElementById('grid-size')!.textContent = `${this.gridSize}x${this.gridSize}`
...
const gridDisplay = document.getElementById('grid-display')!
const goalDisplay = document.getElementById('iron-snake-goal-display')!
gridDisplay.classList.toggle('hidden', this.ironSnakeMode)
goalDisplay.classList.toggle('hidden', !this.ironSnakeMode)
if (this.ironSnakeMode) {
  const remaining = this.ironSnakeRemaining()
  const suffix = remaining === 1 ? 'pt' : 'pts'
  document.getElementById('iron-snake-goal')!.textContent = `${remaining} ${suffix}`
}
```

Relevant DOM element IDs (from `index.html`, lines 82-100):
- `#score`, `#p1-score`, `#p2-score`, `#level`, `#grid-size`
- `#grid-display` (hidden in Iron Snake) and `#iron-snake-goal-display` / `#iron-snake-goal` â€” this is the existing "points to goal" readout ("To goal: N pts"). This is the natural place to swap in an "Exit open!" state.
- Overlay banners live inside `.canvas-wrap` (line 91): `#starvation-warning`, `#death-banner`, `#rainbow-banner`.

`updateStarvationWarning()` (lines 2275-2295) shows/hides `#starvation-warning` with text and a `hidden` class toggle â€” a simple reusable toast pattern.

---

## 6. Reusable banner/toast overlay mechanisms

Three existing canvas overlays, all positioned absolutely inside `.canvas-wrap` (CSS `.canvas-wrap` line 170). All use a `hidden` class toggle to show/hide.

Simplest to clone â€” the death banner (`showDeathBanner`/`hideDeathBanner`, lines 2300-2308):
```ts
private showDeathBanner() {
  const bannerEl = document.getElementById('death-banner')!
  bannerEl.textContent = `${this.deathMessage} â€” Press any key`
  bannerEl.classList.remove('hidden')
}
private hideDeathBanner() {
  document.getElementById('death-banner')!.classList.add('hidden')
}
```
CSS `.death-banner` at style.css lines 202-225 (centered pill, pulsing).

Fanciest â€” the Rainbow "FRIENDSHIP!" banner (JOE-7), which auto-dismisses via timers (`showRainbowBanner`/`hideRainbowBanner`/`clearRainbowBannerTimer`, lines 2313-2336):
```ts
private showRainbowBanner() {
  const el = document.getElementById('rainbow-banner')!
  this.clearRainbowBannerTimer()
  el.classList.remove('hidden', 'fading')
  void el.offsetWidth // reflow so the pop-in animation restarts on a fresh game
  this.rainbowBannerTimer = window.setTimeout(() => {
    el.classList.add('fading') // CSS transitions opacity to 0
    this.rainbowBannerTimer = window.setTimeout(() => this.hideRainbowBanner(), RAINBOW_BANNER_FADE_MS)
  }, RAINBOW_BANNER_MS)
}
```
It is called on game start at line 1360 (`if (this.rainbowSnake) this.showRainbowBanner()`), hidden on screen change at line 2679, and its timers/fields are declared at lines 237-240. CSS for `.rainbow-banner` / `.rainbow-arc` / `.rainbow-text` and the `friendship-pop` / `friendship-flash` keyframes are at style.css lines 230-292. The markup (arc + text divs) is `index.html` lines 95-98.

For an "Exit open â€” reach the door!" announcement, the timed auto-dismiss pattern of `showRainbowBanner` (pop in, hold, fade) or the plain `showDeathBanner` toggle are both directly reusable; you would add a new `#exit-banner` element in `.canvas-wrap` and a matching CSS block, plus show/hide methods mirroring these.

---

## Key line-number index

- `draw()` â€” 735-861; food push 764; depth sort 787-789; food draw case 843-845
- `drawGroundPlane` 348-376; `drawGridLines` 378-429; `drawGroundBorder` 431-485 (border-edge detection 454-484)
- `toIso` 273-282; `isoCache` build 338-345; `getBlockHeight` 487-493
- `drawBlockShadow` 495-511; `drawBlock` 513-524; `drawPrism` 530-579
- `drawSnakeHead` 588-626; `drawSnakeTail` 640-661; `rainbowFaces` 730-733
- Final-fruit colors const 59-63; food color logic in draw 815-818; `ironSnakeRemaining` 1865-1869

## JOE-9 "Add Exit" â€” done

In **Iron Snake** mode, reaching a level's point goal no longer advances instantly. Instead a glowing **cyan exit door** appears on a border cell; steering the snake's head onto it triggers the existing board-morph into the next level.

**Behavior**
- Goal reached â†’ door opens on a random rim cell (never on the snake's doorstep). Fallback advances instantly if the board offers no rim cell, so a level can't stall.
- **Fruit is hidden and inert** during the exit run, and **starvation is paused** so the trek is never a death sentence (per your choices).
- Full signaling: pulsing door cell + HUD switches to **"Reach the exit!"** + a brief **"đźšŞ Exit open â€” reach the door!"** banner that fades.
- Works in single-player, 2-player (shared door, either snake), and bot/bvb (bots retarget to the door so games don't stall). Renders correctly in both isometric and overhead views.

**Files** (`+279 / -16`)
- `src/main.ts` â€” exit state, `openExit`/`isBorderCell`/`botTarget`, tick-loop hooks, door rendering, HUD, banner methods, reset/teardown, `?ironExit=1` test hook.
- `index.html` + `src/style.css` â€” the `#exit-banner` element and its styles (reusing the existing pop/fade keyframes).
- `tests/e2e/iron-snake.spec.ts` â€” 3 new tests.
